dossier_layering.canon_public: Grounded directly in baseline NPC record plus linked polity/institution/locality support where available.
dossier_layering.gm_hidden: Conservative connective tissue derived from agenda, pressure, role logic, and relationship web; not promoted above asserted canon.

core_identity.hidden_identity: None explicitly asserted; hidden layer concerns pressure, leverage, and misread motive rather than a confirmed second identity.
core_identity.ethical_profile: Role-shaped ethics anchored to place, obligation, and consequence rather than free-floating abstraction.
core_identity.general_temperament: Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Sheresh Communes, that reads as someone curious, controlled, and alert to magical contamination, speaking like someone used to careful containment, and the habits of a glyph observer still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
core_identity.short_npc_pitch: Neren Snowveil is a glyph observer anchored to Hollow Veil Shelter, where daily institutional pressures make them useful, risky, or both in play.
core_identity.public_role: Neren Snowveil is a glyph observer anchored to Hollow Veil Shelter, where daily institutional pressures make them useful

setting_placement.location_setting: Humans, Elves, and Halflings are resolved as the core, with Half-Elves retained as a conservative adjacency-based secondary race.
who_they_answer_to: the procedural demands of Hollow Veil Shelter
who_depends_on_them: the continued function of Hollow Veil Shelter

public_vs_private.public_face: Known locally through day-to-day work as glyph observer. Neren Snowveil carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.
public_vs_private.common_first_impression: Neren Snowveil presents as Moves through conversation like someone who solves visible problems first and interprets meaning second. In Sheresh Communes, that reads as someone curious, controlled, and alert to magical contamination, speaking like someone used to careful containment, and the habits of a glyph observer still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
visible_flaws: can widen small matters through procedure
public_symbols: Neren Snowveil carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia.
public_vs_private.private_truth: Keep Hollow Veil Shelter workable through the obligations of glyph observer while managing the immediate pressure of contamination strain in Sheresh Communes. Must hold local credibility together even when the causes of strain sit elsewhere. Around Hollow Veil Shelter, that usually means carrying contamination strain, calendar contradiction, tundra hazard without letting the role of glyph observer visibly slip.
public_vs_private.what_they_hide: Must hold local credibility together even when the causes of strain sit elsewhere. Around Hollow Veil Shelter, that usually means carrying contamination strain, calendar contradiction, tundra hazard without letting the role of glyph observer visibly slip.
public_vs_private.what_they_fear_being_known_for: The degree to which their usefulness depends on strain staying barely manageable.
public_vs_private.what_they_are_wrong_about: They may overestimate how much control role and procedure can preserve once pressure crosses a threshold.
public_vs_private.what_they_refuse_to_admit_to_themselves: That some of their identity is now fused to crisis-management rather than to the clean ideal of the role.

psychology.what_they_want_most: Useful clarity, respect for Glyph Observer, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Sheresh Communes.
psychology.what_they_are_trying_to_protect_or_avoid: Keep Hollow Veil Shelter workable through the obligations of glyph observer while managing the immediate pressure of contamination strain in Sheresh Communes.
psychology.emotional_drive: Beneath the role sits a need for extra hands on a burdened local problem, legibility, and a world that does not collapse into preventable noise.
psychology.core_wound: No singular foundational trauma is directly asserted in canon; the usable wound layer is role-shaped wear, exposure, and the cost of being the person who must notice first or answer last.
psychology.how_it_shaped_them: It turned ordinary duty into a personality contour: they scan for failure early, trust slowly, and measure strangers by downstream cost.
psychology.guiding_belief: If the system is kept legible, losses can still be bounded.
psychology.hidden_belief: Someone always pays for confusion; better it be controlled sooner than wider later.
psychology.lie_they_tell_themselves: That they are only doing the job and that the job has not already begun shaping what they are.
psychology.truth_they_are_avoiding: Their daily choices influence culture, fear, and legitimacy far beyond the narrow wording of the role.

thresholds.anger: being forced to absorb avoidable disorder
thresholds.fear: cascading failure that makes role performance meaningless
thresholds.power: clear information, recognized standing, and procedural initiative
thresholds.smallness: being bypassed, misfiled, or rendered decorative

morality_in_practice.how_they_justify_actions: By reference to burden, continuity, and the need to keep the surrounding place from slipping into a harder consequence tier.
morality_in_practice.what_they_consider_unforgivable: Willful sabotage of trust, records, access, or public safety tied to their role.
morality_in_practice.what_they_will_absolutely_do: Delay, question, redirect, document, and escalate when place logic demands it.
morality_in_practice.what_they_will_only_do_under_pressure: Leverage reputation, force, secrecy, or rigid procedure in ways that damage future trust.
morality_in_practice.what_they_will_never_do: Act against corpus-confirmed identity or role logic without a visible pressure bridge.
morality_in_practice.relationship_to_good_evil: Operational read: Complicated / Conflicted. Their danger lies less in theatrical evil than in what happens when role logic outruns compassion, nuance, or proportionality.

power_profile.why_they_matter: Neren Snowveil can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
power_profile.why_hard_to_ignore: By enforcing Glyph Observer too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
power_profile.secondary_source_of_power: local familiarity, institutional memory, and other people needing something from them
power_profile.why_difficult_to_confront_directly: Confronting them usually means confronting place rules, witnesses, and downstream consequences rather than only the individual body in front of you.

capabilities.combat: Role-dependent; implied only to the degree their office already carries enforcement, survival, or defensive expectation.

In Sheresh Communes, that reads as someone curious, controlled, and alert to magical contamination, speaking like someone used to careful containment, and the habits of a glyph observer still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
capabilities.strategic_administrative: Performs the ongoing duties of glyph observer in and around Hollow Veil Shelter, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.

signature_methods.how_they_typically_win: By controlling tempo, framing the problem first, and forcing others to deal with the local reality they represent.
signature_methods.how_they_escalate: If crossed, Neren Snowveil adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
signature_methods.when_cornered: They narrow options, call on procedure or allies, and try to move the conflict onto terrain they understand better than the intruder.

systems_impact.cultural_effect: Ordinary people around Hollow Veil Shelter learn to speak, wait, disclose, or hide differently depending on whether Neren Snowveil or people like them are part of the scene.

society_politics_geopolitics.ordinary_people_experience: Common folk experience Neren Snowveil as a point where broader pressures become personal: papers checked, routes delayed, rites clarified, access granted, warnings issued, or burdens redirected.
society_politics_geopolitics.cultural_wound_or_benefit: They leave behind either increased legibility and trust or accumulated caution and narrowed speech.
society_politics_geopolitics.secondary_behaviors: People rehearse their story before approaching; they gather proof early; they watch who is seen speaking with this NPC.
factions_fear: actors who benefit from vagueness, delay, leakage, or unmanaged pressure
society_politics_geopolitics.governance_shift: At table scale, this NPC shifts how authority feels inside Hollow Veil Shelter: more navigable if cooperative, more costly if crossed.
society_politics_geopolitics.geopolitical_effect: Small incidents around Neren Snowveil can propagate upward through Sheresh Communes if they touch routes, records, trade, ritual legitimacy, or public order.

hooks.summary: Neren Snowveil needs help with a problem in Hollow Veil Shelter that larger systems have pushed down onto ordinary hands.
Getting involved with Neren Snowveil can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.

who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break

best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Glyph Observer.
